message:
feat(core,harness): materialize attached CREATE TABLE AS SELECT via source; reject certification manifests missing verification contract

Two unrelated follow-ups that ended up in the same dirty tree. Committed
together to make the sweep bisectable but clearly separated in the
commit body.

crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs
-------------------------------------
Fixes attached-schema `CREATE TABLE <attached.schema>.t AS SELECT ...`
so the source rows are materialised through the origin connection
*before* the CREATE is delegated to the attached connection.

Previously, the code cloned the CREATE statement, stripped the attached
schema prefix out of the inner SELECT via
`strip_attached_schema_from_select(select_stmt, &target_schema)`, and
then delegated the full `CreateTable(rewritten)` statement to the
attached connection via `execute_statement`. That meant the attached
connection tried to resolve any table references in the SELECT against
*its own* schema. If the referenced tables lived in the origin schema
— the common case for a cross-schema copy — resolution failed and the
CTAS errored out.

The new path:

1. Detects `CreateTableBody::AsSelect(select_stmt)` *before* cloning
   and rewriting the statement.
2. Calls `self.materialize_create_table_as_select_source_rows(select_stmt, params)`
   to run the SELECT on the origin connection, producing a concrete
   `Vec<Row>` that already resolves the right-hand side against the
   live origin schema.
3. Calls `self.infer_create_table_as_select_column_infos(select_stmt, &source_rows)`
   to derive the column metadata from the materialised rows.
4. Opens the attached connection and calls the existing
   `execute_materialized_create_table_as_select_statement(&rewritten, col_infos, &source_rows)`
   helper to actually build and populate the table. The rewritten
   statement has already had its schema prefix stripped so the
   attached-side CREATE sees a plain table name, and the materialised
   rows are passed in as a value slice instead of being recomputed on
   the attached side.
5. Returns `Ok(Vec::new())` as the delegation result since the CTAS
   itself does not produce a row set.

Non-CTAS attached-schema CREATE TABLE statements still go through the
previous `with_attached_connection + execute_statement` path unchanged.
Also drops the now-dead
`strip_attached_schema_from_select(select_stmt, &target_schema)`
mutation from the rewrite path — the select stmt is no longer sent to
the attached connection, so there is nothing to strip.

crates/fsqlite-harness/src/release_certificate.rs
-------------------------------------------------
Tightens the Track G certification verdict so a manifest that is
present but missing its verification-contract evidence is rejected
instead of being silently treated as "no contract required".

* `CertificationEvidenceStatus` grows a new
  `verification_contract_present: bool` field. Captured in
  `build_certification_evidence_status` by checking
  `artifact_manifest.and_then(|m| m.verification_contract.as_ref()).is_some()`.
* `build_certificate` now appends an `UnresolvedRisk` with
  `source: "verification_contract"`, `severity: "High"`, and a
  "Artifact manifest is present but verification-contract evidence
  is missing." description whenever a manifest is present without
  the contract.
* `determine_verdict` returns `CertificateVerdict::Rejected` for the
  same condition, alongside the existing rejections for a failed
  artifact-manifest gate and a failed final gate.
* Updates the fixture in the existing
  `triage_line_reports_verification_pct_without_double_scaling`
  test to populate the new `verification_contract_present: false`
  field so the struct literal still matches.

crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_2yqp6_7_certification_policy.rs
---------------------------------------------------------------
* `certification_manifest` is refactored to take
  `contract: Option<ContractEnforcementOutcome>` and assign it
  directly to `ArtifactManifest::verification_contract`, so tests
  can construct both contract-present and contract-missing
  manifests from the same helper. `strict_ready_inputs` is updated
  accordingly.
* New regression test
  `release_certificate_rejects_manifest_missing_verification_contract`:
    - Builds a passing gate report (`GateDecision::Pass`,
      `release_ready = true`, `global_verification_pct = 100.0`).
    - Constructs an evidence ledger, canonical catalog stats, and
      clean drift / campaign snapshots.
    - Passes a `Some(certification_manifest(None))` — manifest
      present, verification_contract deliberately absent.
    - Asserts the resulting certificate has
      `verdict == CertificateVerdict::Rejected` and that the
      unresolved risks include the new
      `source: "verification_contract"` High-severity entry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
